questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $4$ identical machines each produce $80\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log_{10}4=86.02.$$

**Answer.** $86.02\,\text{dB}$

$$85+10\log_{10}3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $100\,\text{g/s}$ of a pollutant. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level concentration on the plume centreline (full ground reflection, $y=0$) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $C=\dfrac{Q}{\pi u\sigma_y\sigma_z}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{H^2}{2\sigma_z^2}\right)$ (ground, centreline, full reflection); $H,u,\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times5\times30\times20}e^{-50^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00046619\,\text{g/m}^3.$$

**Answer.** $4.662\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $5\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$ of flue gas. Reading the collecting area $A$ and the drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its Deutsch–Anderson collection efficiency is _____ %.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deutsch–Anderson: $\eta=1-e^{-wA/Q}$, with $A$ and $w$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-e^{-0.1\times200/5}\right)\times100=98.17\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.17\%$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Which device is most effective for removing very fine particulates (< 1 µm) at high efficiency?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ESPs and fabric filters capture sub-micron particles; settling chambers/cyclones are for coarse particles.

**Working.**
ESP.

**Answer.** Electrostatic precipitator

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: When the environmental lapse rate is greater than the dry adiabatic lapse rate, the atmosphere is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ELR > DALR ⇒ a displaced parcel keeps rising ⇒ superadiabatic, unstable (good dispersion, looping plume).

**Working.**
Superadiabatic, unstable.

**Answer.** Superadiabatic / unstable

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Which conditions worsen ground-level air pollution by limiting dispersion?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inversions, low mixing height and calm winds all trap pollutants; a strong superadiabatic lapse improves dispersion.

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Inversion, low mixing height, calm winds
